text.title: market-research-tam-sam-som Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for market-research-tam-sam-som as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LinkedIn employee count × $200K rule used for companies under 20 people or for services businesses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The rule undercounts services and overcounts seed-stage SaaS. It is only valid for growth-stage B2B SaaS companies where sales-to-headcount ratios are established.

failure-mode.description: LLMs hallucinate market-size numbers fluently; they require a URL for every figure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reject any line without a source URL. LLMs generate plausible-sounding numbers (e.g. "$4.2B by 2027") that have no factual basis. The URL is the only enforcement mechanism.

failure-mode.description: Confirmation bias: if the user hints at a target market size, the agent will reverse-engineer to that number.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pre-commit the methodology and share the ICP definition before revealing the hypothesis. The agent must compute the number before seeing the founder's expectation.

failure-mode.description: Currency drift: agents mix USD, EUR, GBP within one report.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Normalize to USD with a dated FX rate in the report preamble. Require the agent to convert all figures before synthesizing.

failure-mode.description: Confusing GMV with revenue: marketplaces report GMV; SaaS reports ARR. Adding them is invalid.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The agent must identify the revenue metric type for each competitor before including it in the competitor-based sizing. Mixed metrics produce incorrect TAM estimates.

failure-mode.description: Stale training data: the model "knows" 2023 market sizes and uses them unless forced to fetch.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a fresh web fetch for any post-2024 market number. Add an explicit instruction: "Do not use training data for market size figures; always fetch from a cited source."

failure-mode.description: Multiplication of fractions: 0.6 × 0.5 × 0.5 × 0.01 generates false precision.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Round each multiplier to one significant figure before multiplying.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring growth: a static $5B TAM today may be $9B at a 12% CAGR in 5 years.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SOM horizon must match TAM horizon. If sizing for 3-year SOM, use 3-year-forward TAM figures.

failure-mode.description: "LinkedIn employee count × $200K" rule undercounts services and overcounts seed-stage SaaS.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Disable for any company under 20 people. Use this heuristic only for growth-stage B2B SaaS companies with established sales-to-headcount ratios.
